content:
Ayush Mishra

Just now

--

JavaScript
Synchronous

• Executes one line at a time.

• Each task waits for the previous one to finish.

• Blocking execution.
Asynchronous

• Starts a task and doesn’t wait for it to finish.

• Other code continues executing.

• Non-blocking execution.
— — — — — — — — — — — — — —
Common Asynchronous APIs

• setTimeout()

• setInterval()

• Promise

• async/await

• fetch()
